title: achillea kotschyi subsp. urumoffii
intro: Achillea kotschyi subsp. urumoffii, also known as Urumoff's yarrow, is a subspecies of the Achillea kotschyi plant, a flowering perennial native to Bulgaria. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Urumoff's yarrow include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing digestive issues. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is also used in herbal preparations for teas, salves, and compresses.
